codeReview.summary: The commit introduces seven new accounting service classes and rewires the controller/facade, but the decomposition objective is not met: nearly all legacy logic remains concentrated in a new 5703-line AccountingCoreService, while the new services are mostly pass-through delegates.

issues.description: Feature requirement was to aggressively decompose the 5728-line AccountingService into focused services, but the implementation moves the god-class body almost intact into AccountingCoreService (5703 lines), leaving the same monolithic responsibility concentration under a new class name.

Target services were expected to own focused domain logic (e.g., journal creation/reversal/listing). Instead, JournalEntryService and sibling services are thin delegates to AccountingCoreService/AccountingIdempotencyService (e.g., JournalEntryService lines 24-53, DealerReceiptService lines 17-23, SettlementService lines 19-29), so the promised single-responsibility decomposition was not actually implemented.

The controller introduces compatibility bridge factories that instantiate anonymous service subclasses with null constructor dependencies (lines 254-370). This keeps tests/backward constructors working, but increases indirection and maintainability cost in a class that is expected to stay thin.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill's decomposition procedure is too permissive against rename-only decompositions; it says to move methods into focused services but does not explicitly guard against relocating the god-class body into a new monolith class.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 3 requires decomposition into focused services; reviewed commit creates AccountingCoreService at 5703 lines and keeps new services as wrappers. This passed worker self-verification despite not meeting decomposition intent.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission convention 'No service class should exceed ~500 lines' is currently not enforced by review gates for decomposition features, allowing a 5703-line replacement service to pass implementation phase checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line 35 defines the ~500-line rule; commit 62b747a0 adds AccountingCoreService with 5703 lines and worker handoff still marks success.

summary: Fail: although wiring and facade thinning were implemented, the feature's core objective (true service decomposition of accounting logic) was not achieved because logic remains centralized in a new 5703-line AccountingCoreService and the new services primarily proxy calls.
